etymology.description: 日(日) (rì, "day; sun") + 報(報) (bào, "report; newspaper") = 'daily' + 'newspaper' -> 'daily newspaper'
etymology.interpretation: 日(日) (rì, "day; sun") signifies the regular cycle of a day. In combination with 報(報) (bào, "report; newspaper"), which involves providing information, it denotes a publication issued every day for delivering news and reports.

examples.chinese: 我每天都阅读日报(我每天都閱讀日報)。
examples.english: I read the daily newspaper every day.

examples.chinese: 这家商店在出售各种日报(這家商店在出售各種類報)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā shāngdiàn zài chūshòu gè zhǒng rìbào.
examples.english: This store sells various daily newspapers.

examples.chinese: 他发表了一篇关于环保的文章在日报上(他發表了一篇關於環保的文章在日報上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā fābiǎo le yī piān guānyú huánbǎo de wénzhāng zài rìbào shàng.
examples.english: He published an article about environmental protection in the daily newspaper.
